name: The Sopranos
description: Tony, Carmela, Christopher, Dr. Melfi — the HBO series that started Peak TV.

questions.q: Who created The Sopranos?
Who plays Tony Soprano?

source.notes: Won 3 Emmys for the role. Died 2013.

questions.q: Which network aired The Sopranos (1999-2007)?
Who plays Tony's wife Carmela Soprano?

source.notes: Won 3 Emmys. Later starred in Nurse Jackie.

questions.q: Who plays Tony's psychiatrist Dr. Jennifer Melfi?

source.notes: Bracco was originally offered the role of Carmela.

questions.q: Who plays Christopher Moltisanti, Tony's nephew?

source.notes: Won an Emmy for the role.

questions.q: What's the strip club Tony's crew uses as a hangout?
choices: All accepted (Bada Bing is fictional name; Satin Dolls was the real filming location)

source.notes: Bada Bing! in show; filmed at the real Satin Dolls in Lodi, NJ.

questions.q: What's the name of Tony's pleasure boat?
What restaurant does Tony famously eat at in the show's final scene?

source.notes: Bloomfield, NJ. Final scene cuts to black with Journey's 'Don't Stop Believin'.'

questions.q: What 1980s Journey song plays in the Sopranos finale before the cut to black?

source.notes: Made the song re-chart 25 years after release.

questions.q: What's the name of Tony's mob captain played by Steven Van Zandt?

source.notes: Van Zandt is also the lead guitarist in Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band.

questions.q: What's the nickname of the pony-tailed wisecracking captain played by Tony Sirico?
What's the meat market Tony's crew hangs out at?

source.notes: Demolished in 2007 after the show ended.

questions.q: What's the name of Tony's pet (and namesake horse) in Season 4?
What's the name of Tony's Russian-themed boyfriend / friend who disappears in 'Pine Barrens'?

source.notes: Famously not given closure — disappears into Jersey woods. Episode written by Terence Winter.

questions.q: What 2021 prequel film fills in the backstory of young Tony Soprano?

source.notes: James Gandolfini's son Michael played young Tony.

questions.q: What HBO show was The Sopranos a major influence on, sharing creator David Chase's network?
choices: All HBO Peak TV that followed (commonly cited as Sopranos' lineage)
Mad Men was directly created by a Sopranos writer (Matthew Weiner)
Both Mad Men and Boardwalk Empire — by Sopranos writers

source.notes: Matthew Weiner (Mad Men) and Terence Winter (Boardwalk Empire) both cut their teeth on The Sopranos.

questions.q: How many seasons did The Sopranos run?

source.notes: 1999-2007. Final season was split into two parts (S6A 2006, S6B 2007).

questions.q: What's the name of the FBI informant in Season 1 (Tony's longtime friend nicknamed Big Pussy)?

source.notes: Killed by Tony, Silvio, and Paulie in S2 finale, body dumped at sea.

questions.q: Which Sopranos child becomes a doctor by the show's end?

source.notes: Played by Jamie-Lynn Sigler. A.J. is the screwup younger brother.
